subheadline: Everything teams ask before adopting Desiderio — including the one about money.

items.question: Is Desiderio really free?
items.answer: Yes — the Community tier is €0 forever. GPL-2.0, all 244 content elements, full source on GitHub, community support. No license key, no feature gate, no trial clock. The paid tiers buy you the creators' time and guarantees, never withheld features.

items.question: What does Pro add for €49/month?
items.answer: Pro (€49/month or €490/year — two months free) adds priority email support with a 2-day response, guaranteed LTS compatibility updates, early access to new element drops and preset packs, and minor-version upgrade assistance. Agencies running many projects take the Agency tier: €149/month for unlimited projects, 4-hour priority response, and a direct line to the maintainers.

items.question: Can the creators host and run our site?
items.answer: Yes. Managed TYPO3 hosting starts at €99/month — updates, daily backups, monitoring, EU datacenter. Hosting Business (€179/month) adds staging, a 99.9% SLA, and CDN. The people who built the theme layer keep it running; hard to get closer to the source than that.

items.question: What if we need an element that doesn't exist yet?
items.answer: The creators build custom content elements from €390 per element — schema, template, backend preview, and the same token-based theming as the other 255, so it passes the audit and survives every preset switch. Brand-adapted theme presets start at €1,990.

items.question: Can I switch themes later without redoing content?
items.answer: Yes. The 15 shadcn presets only change design tokens and CSS, never stored content. Switch the sitewide preset — or give a single page subtree its own — at any point, and every headline, image, and link stays exactly as entered. Redesigns stop being migrations.

items.question: Is it accessible?
items.answer: Accessibility is built into the markup: semantic landmarks, correct heading order, visible focus states, required alt-text fields, and WCAG 2.2-checked contrast on every preset. An 11-category template audit with zero tolerance runs in CI, so regressions are caught before release — not by your users.
